meta.title: Janibacter anophelis sp. nov., isolated from the midgut of Anopheles arabiensis
meta.journalTitle: International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary Microbiology

abstractHtml: A Gram-positive, aerobic, non-motile strain, H2.16BT, isolated from the midgut of the mosquito Anopheles arabiensis was investigated using a polyphasic approach. On the basis of 16S rRNA gene sequence similarity studies, strain H2.16BT was shown to belong to the genus Janibacter, being most closely related to Janibacter melonis (98·3 %), Janibacter terrae (98·5 %) and Janibacter limosus (98·5 %). Chemotaxonomic data (meso-diaminopimelic acid as the diagnostic diamino acid in the cell wall and major fatty acids of iso-C16 : 0, C17 : 1ω8c and C17 : 0) supported the allocation of the strain to the genus Janibacter. The results of DNADNA hybridization and physiological and biochemical tests allowed the genotypic and phenotypic differentiation of strain H2.16BT from closely related species. Thus, H2.16BT represents a novel species of the genus Janibacter, for which the name Janibacter anophelis sp. nov. is proposed. The type strain is H2.16BT (=CCUG 49715T=CIP 108728T).
